2603.20925.title: Profit is the Red Team: Stress-Testing Agents in Strategic Economic Interactions
2603.20925.paper_summary: As agentic systems move into real-world deployments, their decisions increasingly depend on external inputs such as retrieved content, tool outputs, and information provided by other actors. When these inputs can be strategically shaped by adversaries, the relevant security risk extends beyond a fixed library of prompt attacks to adaptive strategies that steer agents toward unfavorable outcomes. We propose profit-driven red teaming, a stress-testing protocol that replaces handcrafted attacks with a learned opponent trained to maximize its profit using only scalar outcome feedback. The protocol requires no LLM-as-judge scoring, attack labels, or attack taxonomy, and is designed for structured settings with auditable outcomes. We instantiate it in a lean arena of four canonical economic interactions, which provide a controlled testbed for adaptive exploitability. In controlled experiments, agents that appear strong against static baselines become consistently exploitable under profit-optimized pressure, and the learned opponent discovers probing, anchoring, and deceptive commitments without explicit instruction. We then distill exploit episodes into concise prompt rules for the agent, which make most previously observed failures ineffective and substantially improve target performance. These results suggest that profit-driven red-team data can provide a practical route to improving robustness in structured agent settings with auditable outcomes.
2603.20925.paper_summary_zh: 隨著代理系統進入實際部署，它們的決策越來越依賴於外部輸入，例如檢索到的內容、工具輸出和其他行為者提供的信息。當這些輸入可以被對手戰略性地塑造時，相關的安全風險就超出了固定的提示攻擊庫，轉向了引導代理朝向不利結果的適應性策略。我們提出了以利潤為驅動的紅隊測試，這是一種壓力測試協議，將手工製作的攻擊替換為一種學習對手，該對手訓練以最大化其利潤，僅使用標量結果反饋。該協議不需要 LLM 作為評判的評分、攻擊標籤或攻擊分類法，並且設計用於具有可審計結果的結構化環境。我們在四個典型經濟互動的精簡場域中實現了它，這為適應性可利用性提供了受控的測試環境。在受控實驗中，對靜態基準看似強大的代理在利潤優化壓力下變得一致可利用，而學習對手在沒有明確指示的情況下發現了探測、錨定和欺騙性承諾。我們然後將可利用事件提煉為簡明的提示規則，這使得之前觀察到的大多數失敗變得無效，並顯著改善了目標性能。這些結果表明，以利潤為驅動的紅隊數據可以為改善具有可審計結果的結構化代理環境的穩健性提供實際途徑。
2603.20925.author: Shouqiao Wang et.al.
2603.20925.authors: Shouqiao Wang, Marcello Politi, Samuele Marro, Davide Crapis
